# ── Increasing Cyclic Queue ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Interactive mode — click Analyze, then "Finish & Interact" to start.
#
# Demonstrates a dynamic queue backed by a resizable cyclic array.
#  • Click "add"    → enqueue a random element; the array doubles when full
#  • Click "remove" → dequeue the front element; the array halves when ≤ 25% full
#  • The arrow (↑)  marks the start (front) pointer in the underlying array
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# ── Queue implementation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TwoSidedCyclicQueue:
    def __init__(self, size: int, start_index: int = 0):
        start_index %= size
        self.prev_arr = ['_']
        self.arr=['_'] * size
        self.start = start_index  # first index in the cyclic implementation (include)
        self.end = start_index    # last  index in the cyclic implementation (exclude)
        self._len = 0
        self._max_size = size

    def __len__(self):
        return self._len

    def enqueue(self, value: int, to_end: bool = True):
        if len(self) == self._max_size:            
            raise Exception('No more room to increase queue')
            
        if to_end:
            self.arr[self.end] = value
            self.end += 1
            if self.end == self._max_size:
                self.end = 0
        else:
            self.start -= 1
            if self.start == -1:
                self.start = self._max_size - 1
            self.arr[self.start] = value
        self._len += 1

    def dequeue(self, from_start: bool = True) -> int:
        if len(self) == 0:
            raise Exception('Cannot remove element from an empty queue')

        if from_start:
            result = self.arr[self.start]
            self.arr[self.start] = '_'
            self.start += 1
            if self.start == self._max_size:
                self.start = 0
        else:
            self.end -= 1
            if self.end == -1:
                self.end = self._max_size - 1
            result = self.arr[self.end]
            self.arr[self.end] = '_'
        self._len -= 1

        return result

class ImprovedQueue:
    """
    An increasing\decreasing queue, but all actions are in O(1)
    """

    def __init__(self, initial_size: int):
        self._cur_queue = TwoSidedCyclicQueue(initial_size)
        self._prev_queue = TwoSidedCyclicQueue(initial_size)
        self._max_size = initial_size
        self._len = 0

    def __len__(self):
        return self._len

    def enqueue(self, value: int):
        if len(self) == self._max_size:
            assert len(self._prev_queue) == 0
            index = self._cur_queue.end
            if index == 0:
                index += self._max_size
            self._prev_queue = self._cur_queue
            self._max_size *= 2
            print(self._prev_queue.end)
            self._cur_queue = TwoSidedCyclicQueue(self._max_size, start_index = index)
        
        if len(self._prev_queue) > 0:
            elem = self._prev_queue.dequeue(from_start = False)
            self._cur_queue.enqueue(elem, to_end = False)

        self._cur_queue.enqueue(value)
        self._len += 1

    def dequeue(self) -> int:
        if len(self) == 0:
            raise Exception('Cannot remove element from an empty queue')
        
        if len(self._prev_queue) > 0:
            result = self._prev_queue.dequeue()
        else:
            result = self._cur_queue.dequeue()
        
        if len(self._prev_queue) > 0:
            elem = self._prev_queue.dequeue(from_start = False)
            self._cur_queue.enqueue(elem, to_end = False)
            
        self._len -= 1

        if len(self) <= self._max_size // 4:
            assert len(self._prev_queue) == 0
            self._prev_queue = self._cur_queue
            self._max_size //= 2
            self._cur_queue = TwoSidedCyclicQueue(self._max_size)

queue = ImprovedQueue(2)

# @viz
import random

# ── UI classes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class LabeledButton(Panel):
    def __init__(self, label: str, color: tuple[int, int, int], on_click: callable, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        dimension = {'width': kwargs.get('width', 1), 'height': kwargs.get('height', 1)}
        self.rect = Rect(**dimension, color=color)
        self.rect.on_click = on_click
        self.add(
            self.rect,
            Label(label=label, **dimension)
        )

# ── Event handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

elements = list(range(100))
panel = Panel(x=2, y=2)
def add_event(x: int, y: int):
    elem = elements.pop(random.randint(0, len(elements)-1))
    no_debug(queue.enqueue)(elem)

def remove_event(x: int, y: int):
    result = no_debug(queue.dequeue)()
    print(f'poped {result}')
    elements.append(result)

# ── Visual elements ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

panel.add(
    LabeledButton(label='add', width=2, color=(150,100,150), on_click=add_event),
    Label(label=V('len(queue)'), x=3),
    LabeledButton(label='remove', x=5, width=2, color=(100,150,150), on_click=remove_event),
    Array(cells=V('queue._prev_queue.arr'), y=2),
    Arrow(angle=Arrow.UP, x=V('queue._prev_queue.start'), y=3),
    Array(cells=V('queue._cur_queue.arr'), y=4),
    Arrow(angle=Arrow.UP, x=V('queue._cur_queue.start'), y=5, color=(140,110,180)),
)
# @end

content.text: Implement a queue that uses O(n) space and supports O(1) amortized enqueue and dequeue, even as the number of elements grows and shrinks.
The queue is backed by a cyclic array with two pointers:
(back, exclusive). Both advance cyclically, wrapping around when they reach the array boundary.
When the array is full, it is unrolled and
. When usage drops to ≤ 25%, it is
. Each element causes at most one resize, so both operations are O(1) amortized.
